label: CD4 T cell
evidence: The atlas paper (10.1038/s41586-024-08002-x) identifies T cells as emerging later in prenatal skin, from around 10 PCW, accompanying thymus formation. Single-cell TCR sequencing was used to resolve T cell subsets. The atlas uses broad annotations including 'T cells' as part of the immune compartment. Supplementary Table 3 contains fine-grained annotations. Suo et al. 2022 (DOI:10.1126/science.abo0510), referenced by the atlas, identifies prenatal conventional T cells (CD4+ T, CD8+ T, and Tregs) across organs including skin. Dhariwala et al. 2020 provides detailed characterization of CD4+ T cells in human fetal skin using CyTOF and flow cytometry.
